trade.summary: No official bilateral trade exists. Arabia Saudita does not recognize Israele, and no formal trade agreement is in place. However, indirect trade through third countries and free zones occurs. The Abraham Accords era generated behind-the-scenes economic discussions, but these stalled after October 2023.

diplomatic.summary: Arabia Saudita and Israele have no formal diplomatic relations. However, the two countries had been moving toward normalization under Stati Uniti brokerage in 2023, with significant backchannel contact. The October 7, 2023 Hamas attack and subsequent Gaza war put normalization talks on hold. Arabia Saudita publicly demanded a credible path to Palestinian statehood as a precondition. As of early 2026, normalization remains possible but distant. Clandestine intelligence and security cooperation on Iran continues.
recent_incidents: Arabia Saudita called for ceasefire and Palestinian state 2024
Continued behind-the-scenes Iran-focused intelligence sharing reported

military.summary: No formal military ties. However, both countries share a common threat perception of Iran, and this has driven covert intelligence and security cooperation. Reports of shared air defense coordination during Iranian missile attacks on Israele in April 2024 suggest deep clandestine security alignment despite no official relationship.
incidents: Reported Saudi radar sharing during April 2024 Iranian missile attack on Israel

financial.summary: No official financial ties. Saudi sovereign wealth fund (PIF) has no direct Israelei holdings. Indirect connections exist through global funds and third-country intermediaries. Discussions of economic cooperation were part of normalization talks.

energy.summary: No bilateral energy trade. Both are significant energy players in different capacities but with no official commercial relationship.

scientific.summary: No formal scientific cooperation. Saudi Vision 2030 has attracted some Israelei tech entrepreneurs through third-country channels, but nothing official.
